questions.question: What does it mean for two word embeddings to be close together in vector space?
options: They appear next to each other in text
They have similar semantic meaning and contexts
They have the same length vector
They were trained on the same corpus

questions.question: How do embeddings capture relationships like 'king - man + woman ≈ queen'?
options: Through learned vector operations that encode analogical relationships

questions.question: Explain why embeddings must be learned rather than hand-crafted. What are the advantages?

rubric.complete: Mentions task-specific learning, automatic discovery of useful features, scalability to large vocabularies

questions.question: What is the dimensionality of typical large language model embeddings?
How do embeddings change as tokens flow through transformer layers?

rubric.complete: Mentions embeddings get refined, contextual information added, meaning becomes more specific to context
